question: Did the review adhere to pre-defined objectives and eligibility criteria?
answers: nan

question: Were the eligibility crieria appropriate for the review question?
answers:
"The number of patients reaching the set percentage of the reduced scale of the lesion area. We included only RCTs involving patients with HMs or VMs. Trials were eligible if they evaluated polidocanol as the intervention of interest in adults or children and compared it to other conventional therapies. "

"In addition, in most of the references, efficacy was defined as a reduced scale of >50%."

question: Were any restrictions in eligibility criteria based on sources of info appropriate (e.g. publication status or format, language, availability of data)?
answers:
The language was restricted to English and Chinese. In addition,

question: Did the search include an appropriate range of databases/ electronic sources for published and unpublished reports?
answers:
We searched the Cochrane, Embase, PubMed, Web of Science, China National Knowledge Infrastructure (CNKI), Chinese Biomedical Literature (CBM), VIP, and Wan Fang databases for all literature from the database's date of launch up to March 5, 2017.

In addition, only published studies were taken into consideration.

question: Were methods additional to database searching used to identify relevant reports?
answers: nan

question: Were the terms and structure of the search strategy likely to retrieve as many eligible studies as possible?
answers:
The retrieval of subject terms and free text was used. For PubMed, the
following search terms were used: "(polidocanol [supplementary
concept] OR lauromacrogol OR polidocanol OR 'polyoxyethylene laurel
ether' OR aethoxysklerol) AND (Hemangioma[mh] OR 'vascular malformation'[mh] OR Hemangiom* OR angiom* OR malformatio*)."

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers: The language was restricted to English and  Chinese. In addition, only published studies were taken into consideration.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in selection of studies?
answers:
Two investigators (Zhi Gao and Yi Zhang) independently screened the
search results, extracted the data using predefined forms and evaluated
the risk of bias; all disputes were resolved by consensus.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in data collection?
answers:
Two investigators (Zhi Gao and Yi Zhang) independently screened the
search results, extracted the data using predefined forms and evaluated
the risk of bias; all disputes were resolved by consensus.

question: Were sufficient study characteristics considered for both review authors and readers to be able to interpret the results?
answers:
The following data were extracted from studies that met the
inclusion criteria: characteristics of participants, intervention and regimen
details, clinical endpoints, and follow-up period. For the analysis of
efficacy, data on the reduced scale of the lesions were extracted. The
safety evaluation included the presence of any adverse events (AEs).

question: Were all relevant study results collected for use in the synthesis?
answers:
The basic characteristics of the studies are
shown in Table 1. Of these included trials, 10 trials compared the efficacy
of polidocanol with intralesional pingyangmycin, two compared
the efficacy of polidocanol with intralesional ethanol, one trial compared
the efficacy of polidocanol with oral prednisone, four trials compared
the efficacy of a combination of intralesional polidocanol and
pingyangmycin with pingyangmycin used independently, and two compared
the efficacy of a combination of polidocanol and hemoclip with
hemoclip used independently. Patients in 15 trials suffered from superficial
HMs or VMs, such as those on the face, limbs, and abdomen, and
patients in four trials suffered from deep sites, such as the digestive
tract or liver. Among these included trials, 12 trials included HM cases,
seven included VM cases in which five were venous malformations,
and the remaining two were not described in detail. Three trials did not
include statistical information regarding AEs.

question: Was risk of bias (or methodological quality) formally assessed using appropriate criteria?
answers:
The methodological quality was assessed using the Cochrane Collaboration
tool for the evaluation of risk of bias, which covers sequence
generation, allocation concealment, blinding, incomplete data, selective
reporting, and other biases. In assessing the risk of bias, "1" denotes
cases of low risk of bias, "-" indicates a high risk of bias, whereas "?"
indicates an unclear risk of bias.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in risk of bias assessment?
answers:
Two investigators (Zhi Gao and Yi Zhang) independently screened the search results, extracted the data using predefined forms and evaluated
the risk of bias; all disputes were resolved by consensus.

question: Did the synthesis include all studies that it should?
answers:
The basic characteristics of the studies are
shown in Table 1. Of these included trials, 10 trials compared the efficacy
of polidocanol with intralesional pingyangmycin, two compared
the efficacy of polidocanol with intralesional ethanol, one trial compared
the efficacy of polidocanol with oral prednisone, four trials compared
the efficacy of a combination of intralesional polidocanol and

question: Were all pre-defined analyses reported or departures explained?
answers: nan

question: Was the synthesis appropriate given the nature and similarity in the research questions, study designs and outcomes across included studies?
answers:
As shown in Figure 1, two trials showed the same number of effective events in the experimental and control groups, and thus, the ORs were not estimable.

Participant losses were reported in two trials: two participants in one trial were lost to follow-up and other unclear reasons, and four participants in the
other trial were lost because of unclear reasons. These six patients were excluded from the analysis.

In the present meta-analysis, either a random effects model or a fixed-effects model (Mantel-Haenszel method) was used for analysis. Heterogeneity among the studies was evaluated using I2 statistics. I2 values of above 50% were defined as high heterogeneity.

There was low heterogeneity in all the statistical analyses (I2<50%), and therefore, a fixed-effect model was used to evaluate the data.

FIGURE 1 Comparison of the efficacy of polidocanol with that of other conventional therapies in the treatment of hemangiomas and vascular malformations

question: Was between-study variation (heterogeneity) minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
answers:
Heterogeneity among the studies was evaluated
using I2 statistics. I2 values of above 50% were defined as high heterogeneity.

question: Were the findings robust, e.g. as demonstrated through funnel plot or sensitivity analyses?
answers:
Funnel plot asymmetry was measured to assess publication
bias. Probability values of<.05 were considered as statistically
significant.


3.5 | Publication bias
The funnel plot generated from the studies reporting efficacy and AEs
was asymmetric. This might be a result of the smaller sample sizes producing a smaller effect size.

question: Were biases in primary studies minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
answers:
The quality of the included trials was relatively low. All trials were described as RCTs, but only three studies reported a specific method of randomized allocation. The others only mentioned randomization. Furthermore, none of the
articles mentioned allocation concealment and blinding.

question: Did the interpretation of findings address all of the concerns identified in Domains 1 to 4?
answers:
However, there are several limitations that should be addressed. First, the quality of the included trials was relatively low. All trials were described as RCTs, but only three studies reported a specific method
of randomized allocation, the others only mentioned randomization. Furthermore, none of the articles mentioned allocation concealment and blinding. Participant losses were reported in two trials: two participants in one trial were lost to follow-up and other unclear reasons, and four participants in the other trial were lost because of
unclear reasons. Unreasonable losses may influence outcome. The analysis
of additional high-quality studies will be required. Second, of these
19 articles, 18 were published in Chinese, and 1 was in English. All of
the participants were from China, resulting in geographical limitations.
Third, the outcome measurement varied across the studies, which
weakened the strength of the identified associations. Several studies
used different percentages to determine the reduced scale of the
lesions, whereas others used objective methods such as doppler ultrasonography
to evaluate the responses. This discrepancy may lead to
inevitable bias. Fourth, different forms of polidocanol were used, which
may influence the efficacy. Many studies have shown that foam polidocanol
has the advantage of better and longer lasting contact of the
sclerosing agent with the vessel wall, which leads to a more effective
treatment and a reduction of the necessary drug concentration (Rabe &
Pannier, 2013).
